hypothesis: A single foundation model pre-trained jointly on multiple molecular representation modalities (SMILES, SELFIES, InChI, IUPAC name, molecular formula, polymer, and electrolyte notations) using a hybrid Transformer-Mamba-2 encoder-decoder architecture will produce unified molecular embeddings that outperform single-modality representations across diverse property prediction, latent space organization, and cross-modal translation tasks because the multimodal training forces the model to learn a shared chemical semantic space that captures complementary structural and chemical information encoded by each representation format.
